title: Silky Smooth Korean Soya Milk Tang
description: A traditional Korean dish from the Gangwon region, this Silky Smooth Korean Soya Milk Tang is a bitter and velvety monsoon-inspired recipe featuring soya milk, made shojin-friendly and emulsified to perfection, it is a unique and flavorful form of tang.
instructions: Rinse the dried kombu seaweed sheets in cold water and soak them in 1 cup of water for at least 4 hours or overnight.
Drain and rinse the kombu seaweed sheets again, then cut them into small pieces and set aside.
In a blender or food processor, combine the soya milk, sesame oil, garlic, ginger, soy sauce, rice vinegar, and toasted sesame seeds, and blend until smooth.
In a large saucepan, combine the blended soya milk mixture, kombu seaweed pieces, and salt, and whisk until well combined.
Bring the mixture to a boil over high heat, then reduce the heat to low and simmer for 10 minutes.
Remove the saucepan from the heat and let it cool slightly.
Strain the tang through a fine-mesh sieve into a large bowl, discarding the solids.
Let the tang cool to room temperature, then refrigerate it for at least 2 hours to allow it to chill and emulsify.
Serve the Silky Smooth Korean Soya Milk Tang chilled, garnished with additional toasted sesame seeds if desired.
